refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported in real-time telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 percentile), explicit duty cycle definition, and real-time observability. The refinement adds telemetry visibility to enable continuous performance tracking and root-cause analysis of cycle time deviations. High safety criticality requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or operator control authority.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time for ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Compute p95 percentile. Verify that measured p95 ≤ CT_target. Cross-check telemetry timestamps against independent site observer logs. Validate that no safety alerts or operator interventions artificially inflate cycle time.
sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The Vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-operations within time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-CT-002: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator command execution with latency ≤ latency_budget such that tele-operated cycles meet CT_target.
FR-CT-003: The Safety Services shall enforce safety interlocks without blocking nominal cycle progression; safety-induced delays shall not exceed safety_delay_budget.

FR-CT-004: The NGHE Solution shall collect, timestamp, and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, load-complete, travel-complete, dump-complete, cycle-end) via Telemetry interface with ≤ telemetry_latency.

candidate_dps: DP-CT-A: Optimize Vehicle hydraulic and mechanical systems for maximum load/travel/dump speed; minimize idle time via predictive sequencing.
DP-CT-B: Implement low-latency Teleoperation command path with dedicated network priority and operator feedback loop; minimize round-trip latency.
DP-CT-C: Design Safety Services to run safety checks in parallel with nominal operations (non-blocking validation) and pre-authorize safe state transitions.
DP-CT-D: Instrument all cycle phase transitions with timestamped telemetry events; aggregate and report p95 cycle time via Telemetry interface.

DP-CT-E: Establish a closed-loop cycle time monitoring and adaptive control system that adjusts operational parameters (e.g., speed profiles, sequencing) based on real-time telemetry feedback to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-CT-Integrated: Combine DP-CT-A (Vehicle speed optimization), DP-CT-B (low-latency Teleoperation), DP-CT-C (non-blocking Safety Services), and DP-CT-D (cycle telemetry instrumentation) as a coordinated design. DP-CT-E (adaptive control) is deferred to a future decomposition level pending validation of baseline cycle time performance.

potential_sources: Vehicle speed optimization (DP-CT-A) must not violate Safety Services constraints (DP-CT-C); safety interlocks may limit achievable speed.
Teleoperation latency (DP-CT-B) couples with Vehicle responsiveness; high latency may require operator skill compensation.
Telemetry instrumentation (DP-CT-D) adds computational load to Vehicle and network load; must not degrade real-time control.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via explicit time budgets (latency_budget, safety_delay_budget, telemetry_latency) and interface contracts. No circular dependencies detected. Recommend establishing a cycle time budget allocation table at next decomposition level.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within Vehicle mechanical/hydraulic limits and Teleoperation latency constraints; risk: if CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy FR.
Assumption: Safety interlocks can be designed to run in parallel (non-blocking); risk: some safety checks may require sequential validation, adding unavoidable delay.
Assumption: Network infrastructure supports low-latency Teleoperation; risk: site network conditions may be poor, violating latency_budget.
Assumption: Telemetry collection does not introduce significant computational overhead; risk: high-frequency timestamping may consume Vehicle CPU/memory.
Risk: Operator skill and fatigue affect tele-operated cycle time; high-stress operations may increase p95 due to human factors.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-CT-Integrated by explicitly defining time budgets for each sub-operation (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget) and latency budgets (teleoperation_latency_budget, safety_check_latency_budget, telemetry_latency_budget). Establish a trade-off matrix showing how Vehicle speed, Teleoperation latency, and Safety delay interact. Defer DP-CT-E (adaptive control) until baseline performance is validated.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to achieve cycle time KPI. It owns the cycle time monitoring logic, time budget allocation, and coordination of sub-system performance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution transmits cycle time events (load-start, load-complete, travel-complete, dump-complete, cycle-end) with timestamps to external monitoring/analytics systems. Frequency: per-cycle. Latency requirement: ≤ telemetry_latency (typically 1–5 seconds for post-cycle reporting).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives cycle task commands (load location, travel destination, dump location) from Site Manager or Tele-operator. Tasking includes duty cycle parameters (e.g., load mass, terrain type) that inform cycle time expectations.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services indicating constraint violations (e.g., stability limit, proximity hazard). SafetyAlert may trigger cycle pause or abort; NGHE Solution must log safety-induced delays for cycle time analysis.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI orchestration. It integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services performance. Cycle time monitoring logic (timestamp collection, p95 computation, telemetry transmission) is best centralized here to avoid diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Minor diffusion: Vehicle and Teleoperation must report cycle phase events to NGHE Solution.

Vehicle owns the mechanical/hydraulic execution of load, travel, dump operations and can optimize speed profiles. However, Vehicle alone cannot achieve cycle time KPI because it lacks visibility into Teleoperation latency and Safety Services delays. Assigning cycle time KPI to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Vehicle should own sub-operation time budgets (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget) but not the integrated KPI.

Teleoperation owns command latency and operator feedback loop. For tele-operated cycles, Teleoperation significantly impacts cycle time. However, Teleoperation does not own Vehicle speed optimization or Safety Services constraints. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would create diffusion with Vehicle and Safety Services. Teleoperation should own latency_budget but not the integrated KPI.

Safety Services owns safety interlocks and constraint validation. Safety-induced delays are a component of cycle time but not the primary driver. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would be inappropriate; Safety Services should own safety_delay_budget and ensure non-blocking validation, but not the integrated KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of cycle time KPI because it is the only module with visibility into all three performance drivers: Vehicle speed, Teleoperation latency, and Safety Services delays. NGHE Solution can orchestrate time budget allocation, monitor real-time telemetry, and coordinate trade-offs across sub-systems. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective sub-budgets but report performance to NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it also owns other cross-cutting concerns (e.g., energy efficiency, maintenance scheduling). Recommend clear separation of concerns: NGHE Solution owns cycle time KPI and coordination; Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services own their respective domains.

Risk: Cycle time telemetry collection may introduce latency or computational overhead if not carefully designed. Recommend asynchronous telemetry transmission and efficient timestamp collection (e.g., hardware-assisted timestamping).

Risk: Cycle time p95 computation requires statistical analysis of ≥100 cycles; NGHE Solution must buffer and aggregate telemetry data. Recommend cloud-based analytics or edge computing to avoid overloading Vehicle compute.
Risk: If Teleoperation is geographically remote, network latency may be unpredictable and site-dependent. NGHE Solution must adapt cycle time expectations based on measured network conditions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) cycle time monitoring logic (timestamp collection, event sequencing, p95 computation), (2) time budget allocation table (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget, latency_budget, safety_delay_budget), (3) telemetry transmission protocol (event format, frequency, latency), (4) coordination interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (e.g., cycle phase event callbacks, budget query APIs). Consider introducing a 'Cycle Time Monitor' sub-module within NGHE Solution to isolate telemetry logic and avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-CT (cycle time KPI with p95 percentile, duty cycle, and telemetry visibility) is directly addressed by DP-CT-Integrated (Vehicle speed optimization, low-latency Teleoperation, non-blocking Safety Services, and cycle telemetry instrumentation). The DP is logically sound and feasible. No independence violations detected; each DP component addresses a distinct aspect of cycle time performance.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time monitoring logic (DP-CT-D) is assigned to NGHE Solution, but Vehicle and Teleoperation must report cycle phase events. Recommend explicit event callback interfaces to avoid tight coupling.

Diffusion Risk 2: Time budget allocation (implicit in DP-CT-Integrated) is not yet formalized. Recommend creating a 'Cycle Time Budget' artifact that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services reference.

Diffusion Risk 3: Telemetry transmission (DP-CT-D) may require coordination with Vehicle and Teleoperation to collect timestamps. Recommend hardware-assisted timestamping in Vehicle to avoid software latency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI orchestration. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contained as sub-modules with clear ownership of their respective time budgets. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and map to known interface types. No new modules or interfaces invented.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (timestamps for load-start, load-complete, travel-complete, dump-complete, cycle-end). p95 percentile can be computed from ≥100 cycles. Verification is feasible in a controlled test environment (agreed duty cycle, site conditions) and in production (continuous monitoring via Telemetry interface).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution (top-level orchestration). Sub-allocations to Vehicle (load/travel/dump speed budgets), Teleoperation (latency budget), and Safety Services (safety delay budget) are clear and non-overlapping. Ownership boundaries are well-defined.

recommended_revisions: Formalize time budget allocation table: load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget, teleoperation_latency_budget, safety_delay_budget, telemetry_latency_budget. Ensure sum of sub-budgets ≤ CT_target with margin for contingency.

Define cycle phase event protocol: Vehicle and Teleoperation must report cycle phase transitions (load-start, load-complete, etc.) to NGHE Solution with timestamps. Specify event format, latency, and reliability (e.g., guaranteed delivery).

Establish telemetry transmission SLA: NGHE Solution must transmit cycle time events via Telemetry interface with latency ≤ telemetry_latency. Specify event format (JSON, protobuf, etc.) and aggregation strategy (per-cycle or batch).

Define safety delay measurement: Safety Services must report safety-induced delays (e.g., time spent in safety check, time spent waiting for constraint clearance) to NGHE Solution. Recommend explicit 'SafetyDelay' event in addition to SafetyAlert.
Plan for adaptive control (DP-CT-E): Defer to next decomposition level, but reserve architecture for future feedback loop (e.g., speed profile adjustment based on real-time telemetry).

decision.justification: Refined FR-CT and DP-CT-Integrated are logically sound and well-coupled. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with clear ownership and interfaces. However, the DP is not yet sufficiently detailed for implementation. Sub-FRs (FR-CT-001 through FR-CT-004) require further decomposition to define time budgets, event protocols, and telemetry specifications. Recommend decomposing each sub-FR to the next level before implementation begins.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The Vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-operations within time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target. [Decompose to: load speed optimization, travel speed optimization, dump speed optimization, idle time minimization]

FR-CT-002: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator command execution with latency ≤ latency_budget such that tele-operated cycles meet CT_target. [Decompose to: command transmission latency, operator feedback latency, network reliability]

FR-CT-003: The Safety Services shall enforce safety interlocks without blocking nominal cycle progression; safety-induced delays shall not exceed safety_delay_budget. [Decompose to: parallel safety validation, pre-authorization of safe transitions, safety delay measurement]

FR-CT-004: The NGHE Solution shall collect, timestamp, and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, load-complete, travel-complete, dump-complete, cycle-end) via Telemetry interface with ≤ telemetry_latency. [Decompose to: cycle phase event collection, timestamp synchronization, telemetry transmission, p95 computation]

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within Vehicle mechanical/hydraulic limits and Teleoperation latency constraints.
Safety interlocks can be designed to run in parallel (non-blocking) without adding significant delay.
Network infrastructure supports low-latency Teleoperation (latency_budget ≤ 500 ms round-trip).
Telemetry collection does not introduce significant computational overhead on Vehicle.
Operator skill and fatigue are managed via training and workload limits; cycle time variability is primarily driven by system performance, not human factors.
Duty cycle is well-defined and representative of production operations.
Site conditions (terrain, weather, visibility) are stable or predictable.
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be unrealistic; no DP can satisfy FR if target is below achievable minimum.
Risk: Safety interlocks may require sequential validation, adding unavoidable delay that exceeds safety_delay_budget.
Risk: Network latency may be unpredictable at deployment site; Teleoperation latency_budget may be violated.
Risk: Telemetry collection may introduce latency or computational overhead; Vehicle CPU/memory may be insufficient.
Risk: Operator skill and fatigue may increase p95 cycle time beyond system performance limits.
Risk: Duty cycle may not be representative of all production scenarios; p95 measured in test may not match production.
Risk: Site conditions may vary significantly; cycle time performance may degrade in adverse conditions.
evidence_needed: Baseline cycle time measurement: Measure ≥100 cycles under agreed duty cycle and site conditions. Compute p95 and compare to CT_target. Identify performance gaps.
Time budget allocation: Establish load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget, latency_budget, safety_delay_budget. Validate that sum ≤ CT_target with margin.
Teleoperation latency characterization: Measure round-trip latency (command transmission + operator feedback) under various network conditions. Validate against latency_budget.
Safety delay measurement: Measure time spent in safety checks and constraint validation. Validate against safety_delay_budget.
Telemetry overhead assessment: Measure computational and network overhead of cycle time telemetry collection. Validate that overhead is acceptable.
Operator skill assessment: Evaluate operator training and fatigue management. Identify human factors that affect cycle time variability.
Duty cycle validation: Confirm that agreed duty cycle is representative of production operations. Identify edge cases or outliers.
Site condition assessment: Characterize site conditions (terrain, weather, visibility) and their impact on cycle time performance.
